# Source Card: CALIPER Hematology Reference Standards (I): Improving Laboratory Test Interpretation in Children (Beckman Coulter DxH 900)

## Summary
This peer-reviewed CALIPER study establishes age- and sex-specific pediatric reference standards for 47 hematologic parameters, derived from 566 healthy children and adolescents (birth to <21 years) analyzed on the Beckman Coulter DxH 900 per CLSI guidelines. For a pediatric CDS module it provides authoritative, analyzer-specific normative data and strong evidence that pediatric hematology intervals must be age-partitioned (39/47 parameters) and, for eight parameters, sex-partitioned around puberty. The specific per-parameter reference-interval cutoffs live in the article's data tables, which were not retrievable as verbatim text through the available full-text API and are therefore recorded as gaps below (not asserted).

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: healthy children and adolescents, birth to <21 years, both sexes; CALIPER (Canadian) cohort.
- Assay/method dependence: all reference intervals are specific to the Beckman Coulter DxH 900 hematology analyzer; they are NOT interchangeable with other platforms. Any CDS rule derived from this source must pin the analyzer.
- Lifecycle: published 2020-08 (AJCP 154(3):330-341); methodology CLSI EP28-A3c. No retirement/supersession recorded as of access date.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- None. This source defines normative reference intervals, not red-flag/referral/scope-exit triggers. Deciding when an out-of-interval value triggers referral is an implementation decision for the CDS converter, not asserted by this source.

## Limitations & conflicts
- GAP (threshold not locatable): Specific hemoglobin (HGB) age/sex-partitioned reference-interval cutoffs were not retrievable as verbatim passages from source PMC7403759 via the full-text API; a WebFetch summarizer returned reconstructed table values that could not be verified as exact quotes, so no HGB numeric interval is asserted here.
- GAP (threshold not locatable): Hematocrit (HCT), white blood cell (WBC), and platelet (PLT) count age/sex-partitioned reference-interval cutoffs were likewise not verbatim-verifiable from the retrieved text and are not asserted; retrieve the article's data tables (Supplemental/Table data) directly to populate these thresholds.
- Analyzer scope: intervals are Beckman Coulter DxH 900-specific; do not transfer to Sysmex, Abbott, or other analyzers without cross-method validation (a known conflict class with intervals derived on other platforms — preserve, do not average).
- Cohort scope: CALIPER is a Canadian cohort; geographic/ethnic transferability is not established within this source.
- Missingness: this card asserts only the four study-level facts confirmed verbatim from the PubMed abstract; per-parameter values are explicitly marked missing/gap rather than inferred.
